name: Stochastic Pullback in Supertrend Trend
edge_hypothesis: Supertrend is the #1 promoted family (PPR=4.47) with proven binary trend structure. Current Supertrend specs enter on direction flip — catching the start of a new trend. This spec instead enters on PULLBACKS within the established trend, using Stochastic K crossing out of oversold/overbought as timing. The hypothesis: entering pullbacks within a confirmed Supertrend trend captures better prices (lower entry in uptrends, higher entry in downtrends) while maintaining the structural edge of Supertrend's trend filter. Stochastic K crossing above 20 in a Supertrend uptrend means price has pulled back enough to trigger oversold readings but the macro trend is still intact — a 'buy the dip in an uptrend' setup. This is architecturally different from all existing Supertrend specs which use trend-flip entries, and from stochastic_reversal which uses EMA as baseline. The combination should produce more trades (multiple pullbacks per trend) with better average entry prices.

variants.name: stoch_supert_pullback_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core variant: Supertrend trend filter + Stochastic K pullback from oversold/overbought. 8:1 R:R (proven sweet spot).

components.notes: Binary trend direction from Supertrend (1=bullish, -1=bearish). Proven #1 promoted family.
Stochastic K crossing out of extremes as pullback timing. 20/80 thresholds for oversold/overbought.
ATR-based stop loss and take profit sizing.

variants.name: stoch_supert_pullback_v1_10to1
variants.description: Wider TP variant: 10:1 R:R to capture larger trend continuations after pullback entries.

components.notes: Stochastic K crossing 20/80 for pullback timing.

variants.name: stoch_supert_pullback_v1_relaxed
variants.description: Relaxed Stochastic thresholds (30/70 instead of 20/80) to capture shallower pullbacks. More trades, less extreme entries.

components.notes: Stochastic K crossing 30/70 for shallower pullback entries.

indicator_roles.baseline: Supertrend direction (SUPERTd_7_3.0) provides proven binary trend structure — #1 promoted family by PPR
indicator_roles.confirmation: Stochastic K crossing out of oversold/overbought provides pullback entry timing within the trend
